title: OpenClaw Vertex AI Memory Bank Plugin
description: A guided tour of the Memory Bank plugin — learn how it gives AI agents persistent, cross-session memory powered by Google Vertex AI
prerequisites: Basic understanding of AI agent plugins

steps.title: Project Overview
steps.description: Welcome to the OpenClaw Vertex AI Memory Bank plugin! This project provides persistent, user-scoped memory for AI agents using Google's Vertex AI Memory Bank service. Unlike session-only memory, memories here survive across all agents and conversations. The codebase is intentionally minimal — a single TypeScript implementation file backed by configuration and documentation.

steps.title: Plugin Manifest
steps.description: The plugin manifest (openclaw.plugin.json) declares this package as an OpenClaw plugin. It defines the plugin ID, name, description, entry point (index.ts), and the full configuration schema — including required fields like projectId, location, and reasoningEngineId, plus optional settings for auto-recall, auto-capture, file sync, memory topics, and more.

interactiveActions.description: Click openclaw.plugin.json to see the plugin configuration schema

steps.title: Core Implementation
steps.description: index.ts is the heart of the plugin — all ~1,000 lines live in one file. It contains: (1) Google ADC authentication with token caching, (2) the Memory Bank REST API client, (3) core operations like retrieve, capture, and sync, (4) file synchronization using SHA-256 hashing, (5) four agent tools (search, forget, correct, stats), and (6) six CLI commands. The plugin registers lifecycle hooks for automatic recall before each agent turn and capture after each turn.

interactiveActions.description: Click index.ts to explore the full plugin implementation

steps.title: TypeScript & Package Configuration
steps.description: The project has zero runtime dependencies — only Node types and TypeScript as dev dependencies. tsconfig.json targets ES2022 with strict mode enabled. package.json defines the package as @anthropic/openclaw-vertexai-memorybank, using the OpenClaw plugin system for distribution.

interactiveActions.description: Click package.json to see the zero-dependency design

steps.title: Documentation & Architecture
steps.description: README.md covers setup, configuration, and usage. FAQ.md dives deep into how memory generation works, scoping, pricing, and design decisions. architecture.jpg provides a visual diagram of the data flow between the plugin, OpenClaw, and the Vertex AI Memory Bank service.

interactiveActions.description: Click README.md for setup and usage instructions

steps.title: Full Picture
steps.description: That's the complete plugin! Key takeaways: (1) Single-file architecture with zero runtime dependencies, (2) Automatic recall and capture via OpenClaw lifecycle hooks, (3) File sync tracks workspace memory files and uploads changes to Vertex AI, (4) Smart caching for tokens (55 min) and memory counts (5 min), (5) Configurable scoping isolates memories per user or any custom key-value pairs. Explore the codebase to learn more!
